preview_text:
The model favors Portugal at 35% to win, with Colombia at 34% and a draw at 32%. This represents an extremely tight contest, with the three outcomes bunched within a single percentage point of one another in the model's view.

The compressed spread across all three outcomes signals a highly competitive encounter. A draw appears plausible at nearly one-third probability, and the gap between a Colombia victory and a Portugal victory is negligible. The match carries genuine uncertainty; neither side emerges as a clear favorite in the statistical forecast.

The market diverges noticeably from the model. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both price Portugal substantially higher—around 44-45%—while compressing Colombia to 27%. The market is more confident in a Portugal win by roughly 10 percentage points than the model suggests. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; users should note the meaningful disagreement before placing positions.
